3062	  -- `Γ(X, V)`-action being `restrictFunctor`'s `restrictScalars`-action along `(j.appIso V).inv`.
3063	  erw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul]
3064	  -- The `restrictScalars` action `a •_{restrict} m` is defeq to `(j.appIso V).inv a •_M m`,
3065	  -- and `σ_V a = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a = (j.appIso V).inv a`, so the two sides agree by `rfl`.
3066	  rfl
3067	
3068	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: combined algebra transport
3069	
3070	The two `IsLocalizedModule` bridges (I) `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` and (II)
3071	`isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap` are chained into a single transport lemma:
3072	the localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces on the slice `Spec R_r` (a localization at
3073	`powers f'` over the section ring `S`) is read back, across the `σ`-semilinear section isos and the
3074	base change `R → A` (`A = R_r`), as a localization at `powers f` over the base ring `R`. -/
3075	
3076	/-- **(I)+(II) combined: ring-iso-semilinear localization transport descending to the base ring.**
3077	
3078	Given a base ring `R`, an `R`-algebra `A`, a ring iso `σ : S ≃+* A` carrying `f' : S` to
3079	`algebraMap R A f`, a localization `g` at `powers f'` over `S`, two `σ`-semilinear additive
3080	isomorphisms `e₁, e₂` onto `A`-modules (also `R`-modules via the scalar tower `R → A`), and an
3081	`A`-linear map `h` intertwining `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the restriction of
3082	scalars of `h` to `R` is a localization at `powers f` over `R`.
3083	
3084	This is the algebra core of the gap1 `Hfr` transport: bridge (I)
3085	(`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`) moves the localization across the ring iso to
3086	`powers (algebraMap R A f)` over `A`, then bridge (II)
3087	(`isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap`) descends it to `powers f` over `R`.
3088	Project-local. -/
3089	theorem isLocalizedModule_powers_transport
3090	    {R A S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing S] [Algebra R A]
3091	    (σ : S ≃+* A) (f : R) (f' : S) (hf : σ f' = algebraMap R A f)
3092	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
3093	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module S M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module S M₂]
3094	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module A N₁] [Module R N₁] [IsScalarTower R A N₁]
3095	    [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module A N₂] [Module R N₂] [IsScalarTower R A N₂]
3096	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[S] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') g]
3097	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
3098	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : S) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
3099	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : S) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
3100	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[A] N₂)
3101	    (hh : ∀ x, h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)) :
3102	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (h.restrictScalars R) := by
3103	  have hI : IsLocalizedModule ((Submonoid.powers f').map (σ : S →+* A)) h :=
3104	    isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear σ (Submonoid.powers f') g e₁ e₂ he₁ he₂ h hh

3436	noncomputable def restrictₗ {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
3437	    letI : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, V) := Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom
3438	    Γ(M, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(M, V) :=
3439	  letI : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, V) := Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom
3440	  { toFun := fun x => M.presheaf.map i.op x
3441	    map_add' := map_add _
3442	    map_smul' := fun r x => Scheme.Modules.map_smul M i r x }

3444	/-- **The `Γ(X,U)`-linear restriction of sections to a basic open `D(f)`** (`f : Γ(X, U)`). The
3445	section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)` is `Γ(X, U)`-linear, where `Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)`
3446	carries any `Γ(X, U)`-module structure compatible (via `IsScalarTower`) with its native
3447	`Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`-module structure and the canonical `Γ(X, U)`-algebra map
3448	`Γ(X, U) → Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` (the restriction `X.presheaf.map`). Linearity combines
3449	`Scheme.Modules.map_smul` with the scalar tower. This is the consumer-facing shape of the gap2
3450	keystone (instances supplied by the caller, matching `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`).
3451	Project-local. -/
3452	noncomputable def restrictBasicOpenₗ {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U))
3453	    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
3454	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
3455	    Γ(M, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(M, X.basicOpen f) where
3456	  toFun := fun x => M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op x
3457	  map_add' := map_add _
3458	  map_smul' := fun r x => by
3459	    change M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op (r • x) = r • _
3460	    rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)) r x,
3461	      ← algebraMap_smul Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) r (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op x)]
3462	    rfl
3463	
3464	/-- **`fromSpec`-section coherence** (gap2 transport crux). For an affine open `U` of a scheme `X`,
3465	the `eqToHom`-transport `Γ(X, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤) → Γ(X, U)` (along the equality
3466	`hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`) equals the composite ring iso
3467	`(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).hom ≫ (ΓSpecIso Γ(X, U)).hom`. Equivalently, the section ring iso
3468	`σ = (ΓSpecIso)⁻¹ ≫ gammaImageRingEquiv (fromSpec) ⊤` underlying the gap2 section comparison is, up to
3469	this `eqToHom` transport, the identity. This is the coherence needed to read the gap2-core
3470	localization (over `Γ(X, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`, at `powers (σ f)`) back as a localization over
3471	`Γ(X, U)` at `powers f`. Proof: `fromSpec_app_self` + `appIso_hom'` + cancellation of the
3472	`Spec Γ(X, U)`-presheaf maps (all between `⊤`, hence forced by `Subsingleton`). Project-local. -/
3473	theorem fromSpec_image_top_section_coherence {X : Scheme.{u}} {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
3474	    (eT : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens) = U) :
3475	    X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eT.symm).op
3476	      = (hU.fromSpec.appIso (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens)).hom ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(X, U)).hom := by
3477	  rw [← cancel_epi (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eT).op),
3478	    ← X.presheaf.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, X.presheaf.map_id]
3479	  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom', Scheme.Hom.appLE]
3480	  have hnat := hU.fromSpec.naturality (eqToHom eT).op
3481	  simp only [Category.assoc]
3482	  rw [reassoc_of% hnat, hU.fromSpec_app_self]
3483	  simp only [eqToHom_unop, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_op]
3484	  simp only [Category.assoc]
3485	  rw [eqToHom_trans_assoc, ← eqToHom_map (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf,
3486	    ← (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf.map_comp_assoc]
3487	  · rw [Subsingleton.elim (eqToHom _ ≫ (homOfLE _).op)
3488	      (𝟙 (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens))),
3489	      (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf.map_id, Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id]
3490	  · rw [eT, hU.fromSpec_preimage_self]
3491	
3492	/-- **(gap2 core) Basic-open section localization along an abstract affine open immersion.** For an
3493	open immersion `j : Spec S ⟶ X` with the P1 datum `IsIso (fromTildeΓ ((pullback j).obj M))`, a slice
3494	element `f' : S`, and `f : Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤)` with `σ f' = f` (`σ = (ΓSpecIso S)⁻¹ ≫ gammaImageRingEquiv
3495	j ⊤`), the section restriction `Γ(M, j ''ᵁ ⊤) → Γ(M, j ''ᵁ D(f'))` is
3496	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤)`.
3497	
3498	The proof mirrors `section_localization_hfr_aux` but over an arbitrary ambient scheme `X` (so the
3499	localization ring is the *local* section ring `A = Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤)`, not a global `R`): the engine
3500	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` localizes the slice restriction `g` over `S`, the
3501	`σ`-semilinear section comparisons `e₁, e₂` (`gammaPullbackImageIso`) intertwine `g` with the
3502	`M`-side restriction `h = restrictₗ M ii`, and bridge (I)
3503	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` transports the localization across `σ`, landing
3504	`powers ((powers f').map σ) = powers (σ f') = powers f`. Because the base and target rings coincide
3505	(`R = A`), no `restrictScalars` base-change (bridge II) is needed. Project-local. -/
3506	theorem section_localization_hfr_aux_general {X : Scheme.{u}} {S : CommRingCat.{u}}
3507	    (M : X.Modules) (j : Spec S ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j]
3508	    (hP1 : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj M)))

3510	    (hf' : (gammaImageRingEquiv j ⊤) ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso S).inv f') = f) :
3511	    letI : Module Γ(X, j ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec S).Opens)) Γ(M, j ''ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f')) :=
3512	      Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map (j.opensFunctor.map (homOfLE le_top)).op).hom

theorem unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) :
    adj1.unit ≫ whiskerRight (leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom G = adj2.unit := by
  ext x
  rw [NatTrans.comp_app, ← homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app adj1 adj2]
  simp

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app
    {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) (x : C) :
    adj1.unit.app x ≫ G.map ((leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom.app x) = adj2.unit.app x := by
  rw [← unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom adj1 adj2]; rfl

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=

-- iter-190+ body: Stacks 01I8 via QC-on-affine ⟺ tilde-on-affine.
  -- iter-195+ Σ-pair refactor: the iso now carries the canonical
  -- `iso.inv = (asIso fromTildeΓ).symm` section-level identity so that
  -- consumers (Beck-Chevalley intertwining) can trace through the iso.
  -- See `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md` Decision 3 row 1
  -- (NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL).

input.new_string:
-- Step 0 (gap1 substrate, above): the pullback is quasi-coherent, so its
  -- tilde–Γ counit is an isomorphism (Stacks 01I8).
  haveI hGqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N).IsQuasicoherent :=
    Scheme.Modules.isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec N hV
  haveI hP1 : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)) :=
    Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent _
  -- Step 1: the base map `b` is bijective. By definition `b` is the composite of
  -- the `V`-sections of the pullback–pushforward adjunction unit with the
  -- presheaf restriction along `⊤ ≤ j ⁻¹ᵁ V` (an equality of opens). The unit
  -- component is identified, through `unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` (the two
  -- adjunctions share the right adjoint `pushforward j`), with the
  -- restriction-adjunction unit component — a presheaf restriction along the
  -- equality `j ''ᵁ (j ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` — followed by a component of the natural
  -- isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. All three factors are bijective.
  have him : hV.fromSpec ''ᵁ (hV.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V := by
    rw [hV.fromSpec_preimage_self, Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange,
      hV.opensRange_fromSpec]
  have hrestr : Function.Bijective
      ((((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).op).hom) := by
    rw [Subsingleton.elim
      (homOfLE (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm))
      (eqToHom hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)]
    exact (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp inferInstance
  have h1 : Function.Bijective ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction hV.fromSpec).unit.app N) V).hom) := by
    rw [Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app,
      Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (hV.fromSpec.image_preimage_le V))
        (eqToHom him.symm)]
    exact (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp inferInstance
  have h2 : Function.Bijective ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hV.fromSpec).hom.app N)
      (hV.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ V)).hom) := by
    refine Function.bijective_iff_has_inverse.mpr
      ⟨((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hV.fromSpec).inv.app N)
        (hV.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ V)).hom), fun y => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
    · simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app,
        Iso.hom_inv_id_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
        AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
    · simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app,
        Iso.inv_hom_id_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
        AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
  -- Unit comparison: the two left adjoints of `pushforward j` have canonically
  -- identified units.
  have hcomp : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction hV.fromSpec).unit.app N ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.fromSpec).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hV.fromSpec).hom.app N) =
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction hV.fromSpec).unit.app N :=
    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ N
  have hunit : Function.Bijective (pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV hV.fromSpec N V) := by
    have hfun : ∀ x : Γ(N, V), pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV hV.fromSpec N V x =
        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hV.fromSpec).hom.app N)
          (hV.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ V)).hom
        ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction hV.fromSpec).unit.app N) V).hom x) :=
      fun x => (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ V).hom x) hcomp.symm)
    have : ⇑(pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV hV.fromSpec N V) =
        (fun y => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hV.fromSpec).hom.app N)
          (hV.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ V)).hom y) ∘
        (fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction hV.fromSpec).unit.app N) V).hom x) :=
      funext hfun
    rw [this]
    exact h2.comp h1
  have hbij : Function.Bijective
      (pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)) := by
    have : ⇑(pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)) =
        (fun y => (((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).op).hom y) ∘
        (fun x => pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV hV.fromSpec N V x) := rfl
    rw [this]
    exact hrestr.comp hunit
  -- Step 2: the compHom ring map is the canonical `(ΓSpecIso _).inv`, so `b`
  -- packages as a morphism of `ModuleCat Γ(X, V)` into the module of global
  -- sections of the pullback.
  have hact : hV.fromSpec.appLE V ⊤ (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)
      = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(X, V)).inv := by
    rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, hV.fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc,
      ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
      Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) ≫
        eqToHom hV.fromSpec_preimage_self) (𝟙 (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, V)).Opens)),
      op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
  let b' : ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V) ⟶
      moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N) :=
    ModuleCat.ofHom
      { toFun := fun s => pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
          (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) s
        map_add' := fun s t => map_add _ s t
        map_smul' := fun r s => by
          have h1 := (pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
            (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).map_smul r s
          change pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
              (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) (r • s) =
            ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(X, V)).inv.hom r) •
              pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
                (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) s
          rw [← hact]
          exact h1 }
  haveI hb'iso : IsIso b' := by
    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
    exact hbij

        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) s
      map_add' := fun s t => map_add _ s t

          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(X, V)).inv.hom r) •
            pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
              (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) s

  let b' : ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V) ⟶ ΓG := ModuleCat.ofHom blin
  haveI hb'iso : IsIso b' := by

    have h : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom b') = ⇑(pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)) := rfl
    rw [h]
    exact hbij
  -- Step 3: assemble the iso `j^* N ≅ tilde Γ(N, V)` as the inverse of
  -- `tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ` and verify the Σ-pair section identity by
  -- `toOpen` naturality plus the counit computation at `⊤`.
  haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by

    infer_instance
  refine ⟨⟨((asIso (tilde.map b')) ≪≫ (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)))).symm, fun s => ?_⟩⟩
  -- The inverse of the produced iso is the composite `tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ`.
  have hinv : (((asIso (tilde.map b')) ≪≫ (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)))).symm).inv =
      tilde.map b' ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N) := rfl

    (tilde.toOpen_map_app b' ⊤)

  have hcounit := congrArg (fun (φ : ΓG ⟶ _) => φ.hom (blin s))
    (Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N) ⊤)
  simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hnat hcounit
  have hid : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj

      blin s := by
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, V)).Opens) ≤ ⊤))
      (𝟙 (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, V)).Opens)), op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
    rfl
  have step1 : (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app (tilde.map b' ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)) ⊤).hom
        ((tilde.toOpen (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V)) ⊤).hom s) =

        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)) ⊤).hom

data.content:
instance : CoeSort (ModuleCat.{v} R) (Type v) :=
  ⟨ModuleCat.carrier⟩

attribute [coe] ModuleCat.carrier

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
/-- The object in the category of R-algebras associated to a type equipped with the appropriate
typeclasses. This is the preferred way to construct a term of `ModuleCat R`. -/
abbrev of (X : Type v) [AddCommGroup X] [Module R X] : ModuleCat.{v} R :=
  ⟨X⟩

lemma coe_of (X : Type v) [Ring X] [Module R X] : (of R X : Type v) = X :=
  rfl

-- Ensure the roundtrips are reducibly defeq (so tactics like `rw` can see through them).
example (X : Type v) [Ring X] [Module R X] : (of R X : Type v) = X := by with_reducible rfl
example (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) : of R M = M := by with_reducible rfl

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
variable {R} in
/-- The type of morphisms in `ModuleCat R`. -/
@[ext]
structure Hom (M N : ModuleCat.{v} R) where
  private mk ::
  /-- The underlying linear map. -/
  hom' : M →ₗ[R] N

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
instance moduleCategory : Category.{v, max (v + 1) u} (ModuleCat.{v} R) where
  Hom M N := Hom M N
  id _ := ⟨LinearMap.id⟩
  comp f g := ⟨g.hom'.comp f.hom'⟩

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
instance : ConcreteCategory (ModuleCat.{v} R) (· →ₗ[R] ·) where
  hom := Hom.hom'
  ofHom := Hom.mk

section

variable {R}

/-- Turn a morphism in `ModuleCat` back into a `LinearMap`. -/
abbrev Hom.hom {A B : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : Hom A B) :=
  ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat R) f

/-- Typecheck a `LinearMap` as a morphism in `ModuleCat`. -/
abbrev ofHom {X Y : Type v} [AddCommGroup X] [Module R X] [AddCommGroup Y] [Module R Y]
    (f : X →ₗ[R] Y) : of R X ⟶ of R Y :=
  ConcreteCategory.ofHom (C := ModuleCat R) f

/-- Use the `ConcreteCategory.hom` projection for `@[simps]` lemmas. -/
def Hom.Simps.hom (A B : ModuleCat.{v} R) (f : Hom A B) :=
  f.hom

initialize_simps_projections Hom (hom' → hom)

/-!
The results below duplicate the `ConcreteCategory` simp lemmas, but we can keep them for `dsimp`.
-/

@[simp]
lemma hom_id {M : ModuleCat.{v} R} : (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M).hom = LinearMap.id := rfl

/- Provided for rewriting. -/
lemma id_apply (M : ModuleCat.{v} R) (x : M) :
    (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M) x = x := by simp

import Mathlib

set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3
set_option autoImplicit false

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory Limits

universe u

noncomputable section

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (G : (Spec R).Modules) (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)

-- Test 1: bare instance synthesis on the coerced moduleSpecΓFunctor object.
example : Module R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G) := inferInstance

-- Test 2: arrow type elaboration with explicit letI.
example (x : M) : True := by
  letI : Module R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G) := (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G).isModule
  let f : M →ₗ[R] moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G := 0
  trivial

-- Test 3: ofHom into the object, with letI.
example : True := by
  letI : Module R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G) := (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G).isModule
  let f : M →ₗ[R] moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G := 0
  let g : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G := ModuleCat.ofHom f
  trivial

-- Test 4: the modulesSpecToSheaf presheaf object is an honest ModuleCat R object;
-- morphisms into it need no coercion gymnastics.
example : True := by
  let f : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) := 0
  let ψ : tilde M ⟶ G := tilde.map f ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G
  trivial

-- Test 5: LinearMap into the presheaf-obj carrier directly (no letI).
example : True := by
  let f : M →ₗ[R] (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) := 0
  trivial

-- Test 6: ofHom from a LinearMap into the presheaf-obj carrier.
example (f : M →ₗ[R] (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) : True := by
  let g : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
    ModuleCat.ofHom f
  let ψ : tilde M ⟶ G := tilde.map g ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G
  trivial

end

input.content:
import Mathlib

set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3
set_option autoImplicit false

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory Limits

universe u

noncomputable section

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (G : (Spec R).Modules) (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)

-- Test A: construct a hom into the presheaf-obj via ConcreteCategory.ofHom from a
-- structure literal (no Module-instance synthesis on the unfolded carrier).
example (g0 : M →+ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
    (hsm : ∀ (r : R) (x : M), g0 (r • x) = r • g0 x) : True := by
  let b' : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
    ConcreteCategory.ofHom
      { toFun := fun s => g0 s
        map_add' := fun s t => g0.map_add s t
        map_smul' := fun r s => hsm r s }
  let ψ : tilde M ⟶ G := tilde.map b' ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G
  trivial

-- Test B: hom_ofHom + bijectivity transfer.
example (g0 : M →+ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
    (hsm : ∀ (r : R) (x : M), g0 (r • x) = r • g0 x)
    (hbij : Function.Bijective g0) : True := by
  let b' : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
    ConcreteCategory.ofHom
      { toFun := fun s => g0 s
        map_add' := fun s t => g0.map_add s t
        map_smul' := fun r s => hsm r s }
  haveI : IsIso b' := by
    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
    exact hbij
  haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by
    have h : tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' := rfl
    rw [h]
    infer_instance
  let e : tilde M ≅ tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
    asIso (tilde.map b')
  trivial

-- Test C: the counit iso composes, and the trace lemmas apply elementwise.
example (g0 : M →+ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
    (hsm : ∀ (r : R) (x : M), g0 (r • x) = r • g0 x)
    [IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G)] (s : M) : True := by
  let b' : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
    ConcreteCategory.ofHom
      { toFun := fun s => g0 s
        map_add' := fun s t => g0.map_add s t
        map_smul' := fun r s => hsm r s }
  have hnat := congrArg (fun (φ : M ⟶ _) => φ.hom s) (tilde.toOpen_map_app b' ⊤)
  have hcounit := congrArg
    (fun (φ : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶ _) =>
      φ.hom (b'.hom s))
    (Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app G ⊤)
  simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hnat hcounit
  -- step 1: Hom.app of the composite factors, and tilde.map naturality fires.
  have step1 : (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app (tilde.map b' ≫ Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G) ⊤).hom
      ((tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom s) =
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G) ⊤).hom
      ((tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) ⊤).hom
        (b'.hom s)) := congrArg _ hnat
  have hid : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).1.map
      (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (b'.hom s) = b'.hom s := by
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (le_top : (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⊤))
      (𝟙 (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)), op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
    rfl
  have step2 : (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ G) ⊤).hom
      ((tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) ⊤).hom
        (b'.hom s)) = g0 s := hcounit.trans hid
  trivial

end

iter-190+ body work (~20–40 LOC): extract a `Presentation` of
`(N|_V).overSpec` from `[N.IsQuasicoherent]` (using `hV.isoSpec`
transport), then apply `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The Mathlib
gap is the per-affine-open presentation extraction (Mathlib's
`QuasicoherentData` ships per-cover-element presentations, not on a
chosen affine open). -/
input.new_string:
PROVED (T12 session, 2026-07-03). The body assembles the union-merge
substrate: quasi-coherence of the pullback (`isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec`,
Piece A) feeds the affine structure theorem (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`,
gap1) to invert the tilde–Γ counit; the Σ-pair base map is bijective because,
through Mathlib's `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` (the two adjunctions
share the right adjoint `pushforward j`), it factors as (restriction-adjunction
unit component = presheaf restriction along `j ''ᵁ j ⁻¹ᵁ V = V`) ∘ (component of
the `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` natural isomorphism) ∘ (restriction along
`⊤ ≤ j ⁻¹ᵁ V = ⊤`). The iso is `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ)⁻¹`, and the
Σ-pair identity is `toOpen`-naturality (`tilde.toOpen_map_app`) plus the
counit computation at `⊤` (`toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`). -/

2871	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2872	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2873	  \emph{schematic support} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the closed subscheme of \(X\)
2874	  cut out by its annihilator ideal sheaf (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}):
2875	  \[

2877	    \;=\; V\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\hookrightarrow\; X,
2878	  \]
2879	  equipped with its canonical closed immersion into \(X\). This realises
2880	  Nitsure's ``schematic support of \(\mathcal{F}\)'' as a closed subscheme of
2881	  \(X\).

  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
  Write \(j = \mathtt{hV.fromSpec} : \Spec\Gamma(X,V) \to X\) (an open
  immersion with image \(V\)) and \(G = j^{*}\mathcal N\). Pullback along an
  open immersion preserves quasi-coherence
  (Lean \texttt{isQuasicoherent\_pullback\_fromSpec}), so \(G\) is a
  quasi-coherent module on the affine scheme \(\Spec\Gamma(X,V)\); by the
  affine structure theorem (Stacks 01I8, Lean
  \texttt{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_isQuasicoherent}) the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
  counit \(\widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)} \to G\) is an isomorphism.

  The canonical base map \(b : \Gamma(\mathcal N, V) \to \Gamma(G, \top)\)
  (the \(V\)-sections of the pullback--pushforward adjunction unit followed by
  restriction along \(\top \le j^{-1}V\)) is bijective. Indeed the two
  adjunctions \(\mathrm{restrict} \dashv j_{*}\) and \(j^{*} \dashv j_{*}\)
  share the right adjoint, so their units are identified through the canonical
  natural isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrict} \cong j^{*}\)
  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}). Under this
  identification, the \(V\)-component of the unit of the restriction
  adjunction is the presheaf restriction of \(\mathcal N\) along the
  \emph{equality} of opens \(j(j^{-1}V) = V\), hence bijective; the component
  of the natural isomorphism is bijective; and the final restriction is along
  the equality \(\top = j^{-1}V\). Thus \(b\) is a composite of three
  bijections.

  Since \(b\) is a bijective \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-linear map (the
  \(\Gamma(X,V)\)-action on \(\Gamma(G,\top)\) being through the canonical
  ring isomorphism \(\Gamma(X,V) \cong \Gamma(\Spec\Gamma(X,V),\top)\)),
  it induces an isomorphism
  \(\widetilde{b} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal N,V)} \cong
  \widetilde{\Gamma(G,\top)}\). The required isomorphism is the inverse of
  \(\widetilde{b}\) followed by the counit. The characterizing section
  identity holds because \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-functoriality intertwines the
  canonical maps \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, U)\) (naturality of
  \texttt{toOpen}), while the counit composed with \(\mathtt{toOpen}\) at
  \(\top\) is the identity restriction.
\end{proof}

data.content:
---
name: horizon-inbox
description: Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
---

The inbox is the durable channel between the human, the Ground agent, the
Horizon agent, and other projects. Act on it through the CLI; never edit inbox
files by hand. Prefer `--json` whenever reading command output for exact ids or
fields.

## Read

- `horizon inbox list` — read the inbox. Use `--json` when you need exact ids,
  labels, scope, author, audience, timestamps, or comments. Use the list filters
  to narrow without losing the overview: `--to horizon`, `--to ground`,
  `--to human`, `--project P`, repeated `--kind K`, repeated `--label L`,
  `--status open`, `--query TEXT`, `--limit N`, and `--comments N`.
- Labels are the release gate. Local inbox items default to `agent-ready`, which
  means they are visible to agents at run boundaries. Keep that default for
  machine-addressed work, and usually keep it for human-addressed notices too:
  it helps later agents see that the human has already been notified and avoids
  duplicate reports. Use `not-ready`, `rejected`, or no labels only when there is
  a concrete reason agents should not see or act on the item yet.
- Audience says who should read the item: empty/general, `horizon`, `ground`,
  `human`, or `project:<name>`. Scope/project says what the item is about.
- Horizon reads general items, items addressed to `horizon`, and items addressed
  to its project. It should ignore other projects' addressed items.
- Kinds: `hint` (normal guidance), `issue` (problem to fix/report),
  `protection` (standing constraint / soft freeze), `info` (a notice for the
  human — something you did or noticed they should know, e.g. a renamed project
  or an important change; purely informational, never affects what runs),
  `memory` (durable agent note or dead end).

## Act

Authorship is automatic: every item and comment you create through the CLI is
attributed to your role (`ground` or `horizon`) from the run environment — you do
**not** pass `--author` (a stray `--author blueprint-reviewer` is demoted to the
`agent` metadata, not used as the author, so the author set stays conventional).
If you are a dispatched **subagent**, add `--agent <your-name>` (e.g. `--agent
blueprint-reviewer`) to record your identity; it is stored in metadata and shown
next to the role in the UI, keeping the author itself a clean role. Items and
comments are also auto-tagged with the run/session provenance (a small chip in the
UI); you do not pass this yourself.

New inbox items use a two-part body:

- First paragraph = concise title. Keep it short enough to scan in one UI row
  (aim for under ~100 characters); do not put the full explanation here.
- Second paragraph and later = non-empty description. Put the evidence, impact,
  next action, and relevant ids/files here. The description is **mandatory** — an
  item that is only a title is rejected.

Write bodies and comments in **Markdown**: the dashboard renders it. Use short
paragraphs and `-` bullet lists instead of one wall-of-text paragraph, `**bold**`
for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, files, and code
(`` `lem:foo` ``, `` `Foo.lean:42` ``). A long unstructured blob is hard to scan.

Do not create an item whose body is only a title. If you need a `[persistent]` or
`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest

input.command:
"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Recipe: building ModuleCat-R morphisms into sheaf-of-modules section objects (v4.31) — the ConcreteCategory.ofHom + presheaf-obj pattern, and the let-fvar IsIso gotcha

Landed in `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean`, T12 run 0010). When you need `f : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ <sections object>` (e.g. to feed `tilde.map`):

- **Target the honest `ModuleCat R` object** `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)` — this is literally the domain module of Mathlib's `fromTildeΓ`/`toOpen`. Do NOT target `moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` via `ModuleCat.ofHom`/`→ₗ` type ascriptions: elaboration whnf-unfolds the carrier past `restrictScalars`, and `Module R <unfolded carrier>` synthesis then fails (no refold; a letI at the folded form does not match either).
- **Construct via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := ..., map_add' := ..., map_smul' := ... }`** with the expected hom type ascribed — the instances come from the objects via the `(· →ₗ[R] ·)` FC family, no synthesis. A bare `let blin : M →ₗ[R] <object> := ...` fails; skipping the standalone LinearMap and going straight to the categorical hom works.
- **map_smul' across compHom-vs-restrictScalars actions**: `change` the goal to the native-action form `(ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom r • f x`, then `rw [← hact]; exact h1` where `hact : appLE V ⊤ e = (ΓSpecIso R).inv` (proved by `rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 ⊤), op_id, map_id, comp_id]`).
- **IsIso of `tilde.map b'` for a `let`-bound b'**: `haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by rw [show tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' from rfl]; infer_instance` FAILS (zeta-expansion mismatch between goal and local instance). Use `inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((tilde.functor _).map b'))` — works even without `clear_value`.
- **Unit-component bijectivity at open immersions**: `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app (restrictAdjunction j) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction j) N` + `restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app` (plain presheaf restriction along `j''ᵁj⁻¹ᵁV = V`) + component of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. When `rw [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply]` fails with "target not type-correct under instances transparency" (`Γ((𝟭 X.Modules).obj N, V)` forms), do `change`-restatement at the type-correct form, then close with `exact congrArg (fun φ => (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom φ) y) hcomp` where `hcomp : f ≫ g = 𝟙 _` — both sides defeq, no rewriting in the wall.
- **Σ-pair trace**: `tilde.toOpen_map_app` (naturality) + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` at `⊤` (+ `homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by `Subsingleton.elim`) give `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ).app ⊤ ∘ toOpen = b'` elementwise, via `congrArg`-chains only.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1; "$HORIZON_BIN" inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
T12/QuotScheme state after run 0010 Horizon: 01I8 Step-1 pin CLOSED; Lane F block relocated to end of file; updated triage

Supersedes the run-0010 triage memory's line about `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (L676) being a stretch target — it is **closed, axiom-clean** (rests only on the sorry-free union-merge substrate; no new imports).

- **Key discovery**: `QuotScheme.lean`'s own union-merge tail (post-banner) already contains sorry-free `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent` (gap1 = Stacks 01I8 counit iso) and `isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec` (Piece A). Also `Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean` has its OWN independent sorry-free instance `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` (its trailing Handoff doc is stale and says otherwise — do not trust it).
- **File layout**: the Lane F chain (`pullback_tildeIso`, `pushforward_isQuasicoherent`, `tildeIso_...`, `pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`, `_sectionLinearEquiv`, `pullback_app_isoTensor`, `canonicalBaseChangeMap_*`, `flatBaseChangeCohomology`) now lives at the END of `QuotScheme.lean` (after the union-merge machinery), inside a fresh `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`. Nothing between the banner and the moved block references it (verified).
- **QuotScheme sorries now 11**: 5 headline (hilbertPolynomial/QuotFunctor/Grassmannian/representable/QuotScheme, all deep), `pullback_tildeIso` (01HQ tensor side, Step 2), `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (01XJ), `_sectionLinearEquiv` Stages 2–6 (needs N1–N4 baseMap-coherence helpers, blueprint nodes `lem:baseMap_*` exist), and the 3 base-change-chain sorries.
- **Plausible next single-session targets in this file**: (a) `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (01XJ along qcqs — may reduce to affine-local sections descent using the gap2 keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`); (b) the N1 helper `baseMap` naturality (pure adjunction-unit naturality); (c) `pullback_tildeIso` is genuinely harder (01HQ base-change algebra).
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1
